channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 people rescued when California pier partially collapses
item.description: A portion of the Santa Cruz Wharf has collapsed and is floating away Monday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KREX 5

item.title: Relief, defiance, anger: Families and advocates react to Biden's death row commutations
item.description: COLUMBIA, South Carolina (AP) — Victims' families and others affected by crimes that resulted in federal death row convictions shared a range of emotions on Monday, from relief to anger, after President Joe Biden commuted dozens of the sentences.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyer blasts New York officials for 'staged' perp walk
item.description: The attorney for Luigi Mangione, who has been accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, slammed New York City officials for what she referred to as a “staged” perp walk for her client last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge has struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing so-called “harmful” materials to minors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules, state and federal laws, outlining allegations of prostitution, statutory rape and illicit drug use, among other accusations.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida boy has open heart surgery after being hit by drone at holiday show, parents say, NTSB investigating
item.description: A Florida boy underwent heart surgery after he was struck by a drone during a mishap at an Orlando aerial holiday show over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officer fatally shot in a North Carolina supermarket, suspect in custody, police say
item.description: Authorities say a police officer has been fatally shot in a North Carolina supermarket and a suspect has been taken into custody.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taylor Swift makes $250,000 donation to Kansas City nonprofit
item.description: The global superstar made a $250,000 donation to Operation Breakthrough, a nonprofit that helps families in the city's urban core.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, NewsNation

item.title: What to know if you want to follow the Buckeyes to the Rose Bowl
item.description: It will be a warm welcome for Ohio State fans traveling to Pasadena after a frigid December home game at The Shoe.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Beachy, NewsNation

item.title: Chat with Santa or find out if you are on the naughty list with these tech features
item.description: You can access the new (and limited!) Santa Chat feature through the ChatGPT app by starting a new chat, hitting the live chat button.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, NewsNation

item.title: Live Updates: Greensboro police officer killed in shooting at Food Lion on Lawndale Drive
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (WGHP) — A Greensboro police officer has died after a shooting that took place at a Food Lion in Greensboro on Monday, according to the Greensboro Police Department. At 10:45 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: Hawaii's Kīlauea volcano is erupting, sending hazardous gases billowing
item.description: “This one came on really fast,” Ken Hon of the USGS said. “What we saw was a swarm of earthquakes as a crack opened up and allowed magma to come from the magma chamber up to the surface.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Napuunoa, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri company creates healthy dog treats using invasive fish
item.description: A Missouri has found a way to turn an invasive fish into multiple useful products, including a new line of dog treats.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, Fox 5

item.title: Incoming Trump press secretary says Day 1 executive order may tackle Title 42
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Trump’s Day 1 executive orders may tackle Title 42. Leavitt joined Fox News’s “Sunday Morning Futures,” where she outlined what can be expected from Trump’s first day in office next month.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Civil rights icon James Meredith is honored in his Mississippi hometown
item.description: KOSCIUSKO, Miss. (AP) — A new historical marker has been unveiled in the hometown of James Meredith, honoring the Black man who fought white supremacy by integrating the University of Mississippi in 1962.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How faith communities can be welcoming of believers with disabilities this holiday season and beyond
item.description: The Rev. Shannon Blosser sees how his son loves church — the music, the singing, the communion. But the United Methodist pastor said his family hasn’t always felt like 11-year-old Noah, who is autistic, has been welcome.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM and LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man faces murder charges in the death of a woman who was lit on fire in New York City subway
item.description: A man is facing murder charges in New York City for allegedly setting a woman on fire inside a subway train and then watching her die after she was engulfed in flames.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gaetz Ethics report finds ‘substantial evidence’ of statutory rape, drug use
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found “substantial evidence” that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid a 17-year-old high school student for sex when he was 35, used illicit drugs like cocaine and ecstasy, and obstructed Congress’s investigation into his conduct.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione pleads not guilty to state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare pleaded not guilty on Monday to murder and terror charges in a state case that will run parallel to his federal prosecution.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teen brothers missing after weekend duck-hunting excursion goes horribly wrong
item.description: Two teenage brothers, Wesley and Andruw Cornett, have been missing for over a week in Northern California after they went duck hunting in stormy weather on Dec. 14.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates
item.description: The move spares the lives of people convicted in killings, including the slayings of police and military officers, people on federal land and those involved in deadly bank robberies or drug deals, as well as the killings of guards or prisoners in federal facilities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione pleads not guilty to state charges in death of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare pleaded not guilty on Monday to murder and terror charges in a state case that will run parallel to his federal prosecution.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The new right gathers to celebrate Trump and the splintering of the Republican Party
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — As Washington heaved over the possibility of a partial government shutdown, leading far-right figures gathered with thousands of Donald Trump’s most ardent supporters and, for the most part, gloried in splintering the president-elect’s party.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nordstrom to be acquired by Nordstrom family and a Mexican retail group for $6.25B
item.description: ntury-old department store Nordstrom has agreed to be acquired and taken private by Nordstrom family members and a Mexican retail group for $6.25 billion, the company said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Louisiana prisons routinely hold inmates past their release date, Justice Department argues
item.description: Louisiana's prisons often hold inmates long after they were supposed to be released following the completion of their sentences, the DOJ says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pickup truck driver killed by police after driving through busy mall store is identified
item.description: KILLEEN, Texas (AP) — A pickup truck driver fleeing police careened through the doors of a JCPenney store at a busy Texas mall, injuring five people before he was fatally shot by officers, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Virginia woman faces jail time after practicing illegal dentistry
item.description: A Virginia woman has been convicted of practicing dentistry without a license, following accusations that she illegally performed veneer procedures at her tooth jewelry business.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, KRON4

item.title: Leap of faith: A few young women in US buck the trends by joining the ranks of Catholic nuns
item.description: PALMYRA, Pa. (AP) — It was a radical idea, and for many years she had been resistant. But as Zoey Stapleton, 24, walked down a darkened hiking trail, steps behind her parents, she counted back from three and “took a leap of faith,” revealing to them that she wanted to become a nun.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4
